rxn_class_summary: Electrophotocatalytic regiodivergent 1,2-aminooxygenation of aryl olefins to oxazolines using water or urethane as the first nucleophile and acetonitrile as the second (Ritter-type) nucleophile
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of trisaminocyclopropenium (TAC) catalyst to its radical dication, which upon photoexcitation becomes a strongly oxidizing species (E*red = 3.3 V vs SCE) that performs SET oxidation of the aryl alkene to a radical cation

key_intermediates.name: TAC radical cation (catalyst, one-electron oxidized form)
beta-hydroxy benzylic radical (65, after water addition)
beta-carbamate benzylic radical (70, after urethane addition)
Oxazoline product 68 (from water pathway, Ritter cyclization)
Oxazoline product 71 (from urethane pathway, carbamate O cyclization)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes TAC+ to TAC2+•, which is photoexcited to a super-oxidant that performs SET on the aryl alkene to give a radical cation; nucleophilic addition of water (or urethane) yields a benzylic radical that is further oxidized (by TAC2+• or the anode) to a benzylic cation; acetonitrile traps it as a nitrilium that cyclizes via the pendant hydroxyl (or carbamate carbonyl) to the oxazoline, with H2 evolved at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: excess water (stoichiometry probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Large excess of water diverts the reaction to 1,2-diol 69 and produces benzaldehyde, indicating water can outcompete MeCN as the second nucleophile; minimizing water restores selectivity for oxazoline 68

probe_reagents.name: methyl carbamate (alternative nucleophile)
probe_reagents.observation: Replaces water in the first nucleophilic addition step, giving the methyl analogue of 11 and supporting the radical-cation trapping step

probe_reagents.name: urethane (ethyl carbamate)
probe_reagents.observation: Outcompetes acetonitrile in the first addition, leading to regiodivergent oxazoline 71 via carbamate-oxygen cyclization, consistent with the proposed cation/radical-cation intermediates

probe_reagents.name: cyclic olefin substrate
probe_reagents.observation: Gave substantial diamination byproduct alongside oxazoline, indicating competing pathways for constrained substrates

notes: Regiodivergence is controlled simply by choice of first nucleophile (water vs urethane); both pathways share initial alkene radical-cation generation by photoexcited TAC dication. With cyclic olefins a competing diamination pathway is observed. Excess water diverts to diol/benzaldehyde, showing the second nucleophilic addition is the selectivity-determining step. No KIE, radical-trap (TEMPO/BHT), or DFT data are reported on these pages.
